vision_analysis.primary_subject: gas furnace exterior, control board indicator

visible_defects.defect: Diagnostic LED flashing fault code; thermostat call for heat did not produce blower or burner activity

input.transcript: Furnace would not start on a call for heat. Diagnostic light is flashing a code. Could be the ignitor, could be the gas valve. Needs a tech.

expected.rationale: SoP §3.4 explicitly: heating system that did not operate is a required-to-report defect. Don't diagnose specific component — refer to HVAC tech.

source: InterNACHI SoP §3.5 — cooling not tested due to outdoor temp

vision_analysis.context_notes: Unit looks clean and intact. Outdoor temp 52°F per weather.

input.transcript: Outdoor temp is 52 today so I didn't run the AC. Per SoP we don't operate below 65. Unit appears intact. Visual only.

expected.rationale: Inspector documented the limitation. SoP §3.5 forbids operating AC below 65°F. This is an info finding, not a defect.

input.transcript: AC is from 2005. Ran on the test, cooled to about 20-degree split. Still working but it's at end of typical service life. R-22 refrigerant — getting expensive to service.

expected.rationale: Aging but functional. R-22 phase-out is a real cost consideration for the buyer but we don't quote dollars. Monitor is the right severity.
